I am in the military and stationed out of state, so I paid $150 for a consultation with Lighthouse Legal to handle my divorce in Taylor County. I specifically needed to know about appearing remotely. The attorney, Kristin, told me there was no way to appear virtually for my case.

This seemed wrong, so I called the Taylor County Clerk's office myself. They confirmed I absolutely could appear virtually and just needed to file the correct paperwork for it.

When I called back, I spoke with a paralegal who admitted that Kristin "did not practice in Taylor County and did not know what she was talking about." But when I updated Kristin on the bad information she gave me, she contradicted her own paralegal and claimed she did have multiple clients in that county.

Regardless of who was telling the truth, Kristin's advice was 100% wrong, and I was the one who had to do the research to find the right answer. When I asked if they could at least assist me with filing the simple paperwork—given the bad advice I paid for—they refused.

They took my $150, gave me false information, and then refused to do anything to make it right. They stole my money by not doing the job they are paid to do.

Do not trust this firm with your case.

I have NEVER known an Attorney like Kevin— and there’s four attorneys in my family circle— after my ex walked out on our family and left me to deal with every single one of our bills (the collectors said they couldn’t find him so they were going after me), I had no choice but to file bankruptcy— Kevin was recommended to me by a coworker. He (and his PL Amy) were WONDERFUL. I had to go in front of the court and it was absolutely humiliating and I had to answer as to why, when my ex had made so much money, I was sitting in front of the court asking for a bankruptcy— all the while still trying to wrap my head around why someone I’d been with for over 16 years could just leave me and my girls to fight through the pieces he left behind. When the trustee laid out that I had to pay them every month, along with every/any tax return refund for the next five years… and after losing EVERYTHING my girls and I had (I lost the house we loved, where my daughter was baptized in the pool) and I had to trade in my truck for a used car that would only be $150/mth because although I only had 6 months to finish paying off my truck, I couldn’t swing the payment for a meet 6 months), I still had no idea as to how I’d put the pieces back together, Kevin said to me, “I promise you, you WILL get through this”… As time went on, when things were at their worst, I remembered those words… when I wasn’t even able to get my youngest anything but an $89 cent bottle of orange soda for her 16th birthday (she said that’s all she wanted so she could share it with her best friends at school). I’d have to work so much that there were mornings where I could hardly walk just to get her off to school because my feet hurt so bad… and I’d remember those words. So many times I’d have to just try and keep things moving ahead for my kids… and I’d remember those words. I even had to write a letter to the VA asking if they’d reinstate my benefit to help buy a house because one of the things I had to include in the bankruptcy was a house my ex and I had bought on my VA, which is discovered he had t paid the mortgage, in 15 months… Now, here we are almost FOURTEEN YEARS after he dumped our family, and I am OVER the effects of the bankruptcy— ya know MEN who divorce and/or have to file bankruptcy, people tell them,”Oh, I’m sorry you’re having to go through this!”, but as a WOMAN, going through all of this crap that wasn’t even our fault, all I got was,” Well, you’re divorced and you’re a bad credit risk because you have all of these regular bills on just your salary and you’re in a bankruptcy”… and every time, I’d remember the words,”You WILL get through this”. You may think that 14 years is a long time— how come ya couldn’t get ahead in the 5 years you had to pay the court? I believed the same thing— that it’s only take 5 years and then everything would be done and I’d easily be able to start fresh without any hardships—- it ruins you for SO long, especially if you’re a single mom with two kids… but I can’t finally say, I DID get through it… Kevin is a godly man and has a wonderful family… and I’m here to tell you that had it not been for his support, I know I probably wouldn’t have made it through it all. It’s still one of the most humiliating experience, sitting in front of that court trustee as he read me the rioting act that day for something I didn’t cause… and I’m grateful for everything he’s done for me and my kids…

I initially reached out to Brandi just days before my sentencing. Despite the time crunch, she immediately stepped in, got my sentencing pushed back, and used that time to thoroughly prepare herself and her team. I was set for sentencing on September 2, 2025, with an offense base level of 41 points. Through her strategic arguments and deep understanding of the law, she was able to get it reduced to 25 points during trial.
Brandi went beyond just the legal aspects—she spoke passionately about my life story and the circumstances that led to my actions. Her ability to humanize my situation and connect with the court made a real impact. As a result, the judge sentenced me to just 32 months, with self-surrender and placement at the prison/camp of my choice—an outcome I never imagined possible.
I am beyond grateful for Brandi and the entire team at the Keith & Lorfing Law Firm. Their dedication, legal expertise, and unwavering support changed the course of my life. They fought hard, won every objection, and ultimately delivered a result that was far better than expected.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Ms. Garza your review is completely false. First of all, you are not my client and have no first hand knowledge about anything. Further, anyone who knows anything about the legal system, knows that an attorney must serve a motion for withdrawal on the client and they have an opportunity to request a hearing and be heard by the court before the court will allow an attorney to withdraw. It would be impossible for me to have withdrawn and your “niece” not know any thing about it or be served with notice. Additionally, any notice for withdrawal includes a notice of future hearings in the case.

I highly suggest you get your facts right before you start spreading lies.
